disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced urine output, marked light-headedness on standing, or inability to swallow saliva (dehydration severity)
trigger_question_examples: When did you last pass urine, and was it a normal amount and colour?
Do you feel faint or nearly black out when you stand?
Can you still swallow your own saliva?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I passed a small amount of dark urine a few hours ago, I feel light-headed standing but haven't blacked out, and I can still swallow saliva.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No genital ulcers, painful red eye/visual change, or new skin lesions (a recurrent mouth-ulcer condition systemic involvement)
trigger_question_examples: Any ulcers or sores in the genital area?
Any painful red eye, blurred vision or light sensitivity?
Any new skin spots or lumps?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No genital ulcers, my eyes are fine, and no new skin problems.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ulcers are small, shallow, recurrent aphthous-type without vesicles, and there is no fever
trigger_question_examples: Were there any little blisters before the ulcers, and do you have a fever?
Are these like the mouth ulcers you usually get, just worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blisters first, no fever — they're like my usual ulcers but many more and much more painful.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need to go to hospital for a drip, or can I sort this out at home?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Assess hydration objectively (urine output, postural symptoms, ability to swallow saliva). If mild and able to swallow saliva, offer topical analgesia to enable a supervised oral fluid trial at home with clear escalation criteria; if unable to maintain intake or worsening postural symptoms, arrange same-day face-to-face assessment for possible IV fluids.
